_comment: operation-router v2.3.5 config. Verified 2026-07-20 against installed CLIs: claude 2.1.212 (SKILL.md supports model/effort/disable-model-invocation/argument-hint; model IDs claude-opus-4-8/claude-sonnet-5/claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 recognized), grok 0.2.102 (only grok-4.5; grok --help confirms --always-approve, --allow, --deny; NO --no-auto-update flag/env/config exists), codex-cli 0.144.5 (gpt-5.6-terra/luna; gpt-5.6-sol removed from models_cache 2026-07-21 - doctor reports unresolved, invoke-gpt fail-closes, Operation 1 Sol paths blocked). v2.3.5 adds the final-worker marker and UTF-8 stdin preservation; routing, model, effort, permission, fallback, and v2.3.4 log/test/package isolation values are unchanged. Do not hand-edit model IDs without re-verifying.

headlessPermissions._comment: v2.3.5 retains the v2.3.3 headless permission policy unchanged. grok --allow/--deny are compat aliases for Claude Code --allowedTools/--disallowedTools. mode=alwaysApprove maps to --always-approve: grok's built-in policy classifies heredoc/command-substitution commands as ask rules, and under the retired dontAsk mode a headless run cannot answer, so the whole session dies with cancellationCategory=PermissionCancelled (measured 2026-07-20, op2-issue3 debug log). --always-approve removes ask prompts while --deny rules still take priority and block matched commands as a graceful tool error (probe-verified). Trade-off: non-terminal tools are no longer constrained by the allow list; deny remains the effective guard.
